data.title: Debenhams raises nearly $54 million in oversubscribed fundraise
data.description:
Debenhams said on Thursday it has raised about 40 million pounds ($53.99 million) through an oversubscribed share placement, exceeding its initial 35 million-pound target, as the British fashion retailer looks to boost liquidity.

($1 = 0.7409 pounds)

data.title: Sweden's Ovzon Q4 revenue rises significantly on higher orders
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden-based mobile SATCOM provider's Q4 revenue rose significantly yr/yr

  • Company achieved positive operating profit and net income for Q4

  • Secured significant orders from FMV and European NATO customer

Outlook

  • Ovzon prioritizes expanding customer base and increasing order book in 2026

  • Company aims to maximize capacity utilization on Ovzon 3 satellite

  • Ovzon sees strong demand in Europe and US markets for SATCOM solutions

Result Drivers

  • ORDER INTAKE - Significant orders from FMV and a European NATO customer boosted Q4 revenue

  • PROFITABILITY IMPROVEMENT - Higher volumes and increased utilization of Ovzon 3 drove profitability

  • REFINANCING IMPACT - Refinancing contributed positively to net profit in Q4

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q4 Revenue

Beat

SEK 277 mln

SEK 248 mln (1 Analyst)

Q4 Net Income

SEK 90 mln

Q4 EBIT

SEK 74 mln

Analyst Coverage

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the communications & networking peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ovzon AB (publ) is SEK61.00, about 19.8% above its February 18 closing price of SEK50.90

  • The stock recently traded at 84 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 30 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: UK's Mondi 2025 revenue up 3% on integration of Schumacher Packaging's assets
data.description:
Overview

  • Global packaging leader's 2025 revenue rose 3% yr/yr, underlying EBITDA fell 5%

  • Company streamlined operations with plant closures and Schumacher integration

  • Company focused on operational excellence and cost discipline amid market downturn

Outlook

  • Mondi expects 2026 capital expenditure to be approximately €550 mln, focusing on maintenance

  • Company sees geopolitical and macroeconomic conditions remaining uncertain in 2026

  • Mondi confident in structural growth drivers for sustainable packaging despite cyclical pressures

Result Drivers

  • OPERATIONAL EXCELLENCE - Mondi emphasized operational excellence and cost discipline, streamlining operations by combining business units and closing plants

  • SCHUMACHER INTEGRATION - Integration of Schumacher Packaging's assets expected to yield €32 mln in cost synergies over three years

  • SUSTAINABLE PACKAGING - Mondi focused on sustainable packaging solutions, leveraging integrated assets and market leadership

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

FY Revenue

EUR 7.66 bln

FY Adjusted EBITDA

EUR 1.001 bln

FY Pretax Profit

EUR 269 mln

FY ROCE

6.70%

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 7 "strong buy" or "buy", 6 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the paper packaging peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Mondi PLC is GBp1,080.00, about 16.8% above its February 18 closing price of GBp924.40

Press Release:

data.title: Sweden's Viva Wine Q4 sales rise 48.5% y/y on acquisitions of Delta Wines, Vinguiden
data.description:
Overview

  • European wine group's Q4 net sales rose 48.5% yr/yr

  • Adjusted EBITA for Q4 increased to SEK 159 mln

  • Company acquired Delta Wines and Vinguiden Nordic AB

Outlook

  • Viva Wine plans strategic acquisitions to strengthen growth in 2026

  • Company aims to grow faster than the market using scale and geographical reach

  • Market conditions remain uncertain, but Viva Wine focuses on profitable growth

Result Drivers

  • ACQUISITIONS - Acquisitions of Delta Wines and Vinguiden Nordic AB significantly boosted net sales and expanded B2B segment

  • ORGANIC GROWTH - B2C segment achieved organic growth, driven by increased customer base and marketing investments

  • MARKET POSITION - Listing on Nasdaq Stockholm Main Market supports strategic growth and is seen as a quality stamp

data.title: Denmark's GomSpace Q4 revenue jumps 75%
data.description:
Overview

  • Denmark small satellite provider's Q4 revenue rose 75%, achieving all-time high

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 grew threefold, reflecting strong operational performance

  • Company faces negative free cash flow due to delayed customer payments

Outlook

  • GomSpace maintains 2026 revenue guidance at 540-640 M.SEK

  • Company expects negative free cash flow for 2026 due to scaling investments

  • GomSpace sees 2026 EBITDA margin between 5% and 12%

Result Drivers

  • REVENUE GROWTH - Q4 revenue increased by 75%, driven by strong operational performance

  • NEGATIVE CASH FLOW - Negative free cash flow due to delayed payments from a key customer, with mitigation measures in place

  • ORDER BACKLOG - Order backlog increased by 13% to 410 MSEK, supporting future growth

Key Details

Metric

FY EPS

Beat/Miss

Actual

-SEK 0.17

Consensus Estimate

Analyst Coverage

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for GomSpace Group AB is SEK8.20, about 61.2% below its February 18 closing price of SEK21.15

Press Release:

For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact reuters.support@thomsonreuters.com.

data.description:

data.title: Zurich Insurance's 2025 Achievements Are Considerable — Market Talk
data.description: References to Zurich Insurance's takeover offer for cyber insurer Beazley are likely to take the center stage but this shouldn't cast a shadow on its 2025 results, Jefferies says in a research note. The Swiss insurer reported double-digit growth in its operating profit and in its core return on equity, while lifting its dividend by 7%. These are considerable achievements, analysts Philip Kett and Derald Goh write. "In our view, if it weren't for the overhang of an impending equity raise, the shares would be up on this," they say. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Discount for Russia's Urals deepens to $28 in the Baltic - biggest since 2023 peak, Kommersant says
data.description:
The discount to Brent for Russia's flagship Urals oil blend in the Baltic Sea has widened to $28 per barrel, the highest level seen since a peak in April 2023, the Kommersant newspaper reported on Thursday citing data from Argus.

The discounts for Russian oil, forced by Western sanctions imposed over the conflict in Ukraine, have hit federal budget revenues.

Kommersant said the Urals discount to Brent on the Free on Board (FOB) basis in the ports of Primorsk and Ust-Luga in February 9-13 widened by $0.7 to $28 per barrel, the biggest since April 2023.

It said the outright Urals price for the period declined by $1.77 to $42.28 per barrel.

data.title: VINCIT: Revenue fell 18.4% and net loss widened, but product business grew and 2026 outlook is positive
data.description:
Revenue declined 18.4% year-over-year due to market challenges and restructuring, resulting in a net loss of EUR 2.9 million. Service business contracted, while the product business grew over 20%. 2026 profitability is expected to improve, with a focus on AI and efficiency.

data.title: Poland - Factors to Watch February 19
data.description:
The following are significant news stories, press reports and events on Thursday. ALL TIMES GMT (Poland: GMT + 1 hour):

DRONES

On Thursday, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk and Minister of National Defense Wladyslaw Kosiniak-Kamysz will participate in tests of drones and counter-drone systems, including the SAN counter-drone system, 90% of which is to be financed from European funds under the SAFE program.

DATA

On Thursday, the Statistical Office (GUS) will release data on industrial production in Poland for January. Analysts polled by Reuters estimate that it increased by 2.4% year-on-year, compared to a 7.3% increase in December 2025.

GUS will also release information on wage growth in January, which, according to analysts, reached 7.2% year-on-year, compared to 8.6% the previous month.

ORLEN

Polish energy group Orlen reported a lower-than-expected fourth-quarter net profit on Thursday, as a surge in refining margins failed to fully counter lower gas and oil prices and asset impairments.

BANK PEKAO SA

Bank Pekao , Poland's second-biggest lender by assets, reported a more than 13% rise in its fourth-quarter profit on Thursday, driven by lower credit loss allowances and Swiss franc legal provisions, and higher fee and commission income.

POLISH ECONOMY

Poland's economy could grow by nearly 4% in 2026, with accelerating investment poised to become a key driver, government officials said on Wednesday.

****Reuters has not verified stories reported by Polish media and does not vouch for their accuracy.****
